verdict.answer_first: A states the definition immediately: "Idempotency means that making the same request multiple times produces the same result as making it once."
verdict.decisiveness: B is more prescriptive, calling the idempotency key 'the standard pattern' and detailing its implementation, whereas A presents it as one of several options.
verdict.actionability: A is faster to act on. It explains the concept, the 'why', and the primary solution (idempotency key) without the implementation details in B that I can look up when I actually build it.
